title: fix(core): capture tripwire aborts in OpenTelemetry spans

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(core): handle TripWire from processInputStep properly
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relevance: Related prior fix for TripWire handling in processInputStep; this PR extends observability coverage for those same code paths

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: Fixed tracing of processors
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relevance: Prior fix for processor tracing; this PR builds on that foundation to capture tripwire-specific abort details

relatedIssues.title: [FEATURE] Ensure processor trip-wire aborts are traced properly

summary: When a processor tripwire aborts an agent run, the tracing spans did not record why the run was short-circuited, making tripwire-triggered aborts invisible in tracing dashboards. This PR changes all tripwire catch blocks across processor runner (input, output, beforeGenerate, streaming), agent-legacy, agent onFinish, and the v5 workflow map-results-step to use span.error() with structured tripwire attributes (reason, processorId, retry, metadata) instead of span.end() with plain metadata. It also adds tripwire-specific attribute types to the tracing type definitions. The bulk of the diff is wrapping existing processor logic in try/catch blocks to properly capture and propagate tripwire errors to OTel spans.
maintainerNotes: Maintainer-authored PR (DanielSLew is a repo collaborator). Review requested from epinzur. CI has 4 failures (Agent Builder Tests, Core Package Tests, Full Test Suite, Observability Package Tests) — these should be investigated to determine if they are related to this change or pre-existing/flaky. No new tests added, but the changes are primarily observability wiring (calling span.error instead of span.end with metadata). The changeset is present and correctly scoped as a patch to @mastra/core. CodeRabbit left automated review comments that the author responded to. The scope is focused and reasonable — all changes serve the single goal of making tripwire aborts visible in traces.
